funding_references.funder_name: FP7-ICT PASCAL2 Network of Excellence ICT-216886

title: BNCI 2015-009 AMUSE (Auditory Multi-class Spatial ERP) dataset
description: The BNCI 2015-009 AMUSE dataset comprises EEG recordings from 21 healthy subjects performing an auditory oddball task using spatial hearing cues for brain-computer interface applications. The dataset includes 60-channel EEG data acquired at 250 Hz (downsampled to 100 Hz) with preprocessed P300 event-related potentials elicited by target and non-target auditory stimuli presented from five spatially distributed speakers. This dataset demonstrates high classification accuracy (90% mean, up to 100% for best subjects) and information transfer rates suitable for auditory BCI-based communication and speller applications.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Brain Products 128-channel amplifier at 250 Hz sampling rate from 60 channels with Ag/AgCl electrodes in 10-20 montage, referenced to nose. Online analog bandpass filtering (0.1-250 Hz) was applied during acquisition. Preprocessing included offline bandpass filtering (0.1-250 Hz, Chebyshev II order 8), 50 Hz notch filtering, downsampling to 100 Hz, and threshold-based artifact rejection (>70 µV on ocular channels). Subjects performed an offline auditory oddball task with three conditions varying inter-stimulus interval (300 ms, 175 ms) and speaker configuration, responding to target stimuli from designated spatial locations via mental counting or keypress.
